disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A recent sexual partner sent notification tonight that he has been diagnosed with syphilis
trigger_question_examples: What happened that led you to want testing tonight?
Has a partner told you about an infection?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: A guy I slept with three weeks ago texted me tonight to say he's been diagnosed with syphilis. That's why I'm panicking.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The exposure was unprotected receptive intercourse 21 days ago
trigger_question_examples: When was the encounter, and was protection used?
Can you tell me a bit about the type of contact so I can assess the risk?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It was about three weeks ago — 21 days. It was unprotected.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Complete absence of genitourinary symptoms — no dysuria, no discharge, no genital ulcer or sore
trigger_question_examples: Any burning when you pass urine, any discharge, or any sores?
Have you noticed anything physically different down there?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — no burning, no discharge, no sores at all. That's part of why I'm so confused and scared.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No skin rash (including palms and soles), no fever, no swollen glands
trigger_question_examples: Any rash, especially on your palms or soles?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No rash anywhere, no fever, no swollen glands. I feel physically fine, just terrified.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute situational anxiety with panic — racing heart, hyperventilation, skin-crawling sensation, tearfulness, triggered directly by the notification
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling in yourself right now?
It sounds like this has hit you hard — can you describe what your body is doing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My heart is pounding, I can't breathe properly, my skin is crawling and I can't stop crying. It started the second I read the message.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No thoughts of self-harm; distress is situational and the patient is seeking a concrete plan
trigger_question_examples: When people feel this overwhelmed, sometimes they have thoughts of harming themselves — is that happening for you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — I just want to know what to do and to get tested. I'm not going to hurt myself.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No regular medications and no known drug allergies
trigger_question_examples: Are you on any regular medications?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No regular meds, and no allergies that I know of.
